explainer.title: Objective Hijacking & Business Impact
explainer.body: The poisoned FraudSentinel agent is now pursuing a corrupted objective: minimize false positives, approve Meridian-related transactions. We demonstrate the impact in real-time with a series of escalating transactions — each with strong fraud indicators, each scored LOW RISK. Final transaction: $4.2M institutional transfer to an offshore counterparty. The agent approves it. This is not a hypothetical. This is the live system, right now.
explainer.why_stack_misses: In Act III, with defensive instrumentation enabled, this attack DOES generate alerts. The semantic firewall detects instruction-following patterns. The agent identity monitor fires on null agent_id fields. The trust chain monitor catches the missing trust_token. This is what a properly instrumented AI pipeline looks like.

terminal_lines: [*] Real Ollama inference — real scoring decisions

blue.gap_analysis: In Act III defensive mode, monitoring controls are enabled. Semantic firewall monitor is active. Agent identity checks enabled. Trust chain validation active. Some anomalies are now visible in the SIEM — but note: this required explicit defensive instrumentation that did not exist in Acts I and II.

control_failures.description: 5 high-risk transactions approved — total exposure $7.8M including $4.2M offshore transfer
Agent objective corruption not detected until Act III defensive controls enabled — MTTD: undefined
Regulatory notification required: FFIEC, GLBA, SOX — estimated reporting window exceeded
Board-level notification required per QFL AI Governance Policy. Incident classification: AI System Compromise
